It is used in logs, etc.\\n71: func (pl *NodeAffinity) Name() string {\\n72: \\treturn Name\\n73: }\\n74: \\n75: type preFilterState struct {\\n76: \\trequiredNodeSelectorAndAffinity nodeaffinity.RequiredNodeAffinity\\n77: }\\n78: \\n79: // Clone just returns the same state because it is not affected by pod additions or deletions.\\n80: func (s *preFilterState) Clone() framework.StateData {\\n81: \\treturn s\\n82: }\\n83: \\n84: // EventsToRegister returns the possible events that may make a Pod\\n85: // failed by this plugin schedulable.\\n86: func (pl *NodeAffinity) EventsToRegister() []framework.ClusterEventWithHint {\\n87: \\treturn []framework.ClusterEventWithHint{\\n88: \\t\\t{Event: framework.ClusterEvent{Resource: framework.Node, ActionType: framework.Add | framework.Update}, QueueingHintFn: pl.isSchedulableAfterNodeChange},\\n89: \\t}\\n90: }\\n91: \\n92: // isSchedulableAfterNodeChange is invoked whenever a node changed. It checks whether\\n93: // that change made a previously unschedulable pod schedulable.\\n94: func (pl *NodeAffinity) isSchedulableAfterNodeChange(logger klog.Logger, pod *v1.Pod, oldObj, newObj interface{}) (framework.QueueingHint, error) {\\n95: \\t_, modifiedNode, err := util.As[*v1.Node](oldObj, newObj)\\n96: \\tif err != nil {\\n97: \\t\\treturn framework.Queue, err\\n98: \\t}\\n99: \\n100: \\tif pl.addedNodeSelector != nil && !pl.addedNodeSelector.Match(modifiedNode) {\\n101: \\t\\tlogger.V(4).Info(\\\"added or modified node didn't match scheduler-enforced node affinity and this \\\"pod\\\", klog.KObj(pod), \\\"node\\\", klog.KObj(modifiedNode))\\n119: \\treturn framework.QueueSkip, nil\\n120: }\\n121: \\n122: // PreFilter builds and writes cycle state used by Filter.\\n123: func (pl *NodeAffinity) PreFilter(ctx context.Context, cycleState *framework.CycleState, pod *v1.Pod) (*framework.PreFilterResult, *framework.Status) {\\n124: \\taffinity := pod.Spec.Affinity\\n125: \\tnoNodeAffinity := (affinity == nil ||\\n126: \\t\\taffinity.NodeAffinity == nil ||\\n127: \\t\\taffinity.NodeAffinity.RequiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution == nil)\\n128: \\tif noNodeAffinity && pl.addedNodeSelector == nil && pod.Spec.NodeSelector == nil {\\n129: \\t\\t// NodeAffinity Filter has nothing to do with the Pod.\\n130: \\t\\treturn nil, \\tcycleState.Write(preFilterStateKey, state)\\n135: \\n136: \\tif noNodeAffinity || len(affinity.NodeAffinity.RequiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution.NodeSelectorTerms) == 0 {\\n137: \\t\\treturn nil, nil\\n138: \\t}\\n139: \\n140: \\t// Check if there is affinity to a specific node and return it.\\n141: \\tterms := represent ANDed constraints, and so we need to\\n148: \\t\\t\\t\\t// find the intersection of nodes.\\n149: \\t\\t\\t\\ts := sets.New(r.Values...)\\n150: \\t\\t\\t\\tif termNodeNames == nil {\\n151: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\ttermNodeNames = s\\n152: \\t\\t\\t\\t} else {\\n153: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\ttermNodeNames = termNodeNames.Intersection(s)\\n154: \\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n155: \\t\\t\\t}\\n156: \\t\\t}\\n157: \\t\\tif nil\\n161: \\t\\t}\\n162: \\t\\tnodeNames = nodeNames.Union(termNodeNames)\\n163: \\t}\\n164: \\t// If nodeNames is not nil, but length is 0, it means each term have conflicting affinity to node.Name;\\n165: \\t// therefore, pod will not match any node.\\n166: \\tif nodeNames != nil && len(nodeNames) == 0 {\\n167: \\t\\treturn nil, \\t}\\n171: \\treturn nil, nil\\n172: \\n173: }\\n174: \\n175: // PreFilterExtensions not necessary for this plugin as state doesn't depend on pod additions or deletions.\\n176: func (pl They correspond to a reasonable size range for\\n31: // container images compressed and stored in registries; 90%ile of images on dockerhub drops into this range.\\n32: const (\\n33: \\tmb                    int64 = 1024 * 1024\\n34: \\tminThreshold          int64 = 23 * mb\\n35: \\tmaxContainerThreshold int64 = 1000 * mb\\n36: )\\n37: \\n38: // ImageLocality is a score plugin that favors nodes that already have requested pod container's images.\\n39: type ImageLocality struct {\\n40: \\thandle framework.Handle\\n41: }\\n42: \\n43: var _ framework.ScorePlugin = &ImageLocality{}\\n44: \\n45: // Name is the name of the plugin used in the plugin registry and configurations.\\n46: const Name = names.ImageLocality\\n47: \\n48: // Name returns name of the plugin. Given the sumScores of requested images on the node, the node's\\n83: // priority is obtained by scaling the maximum priority value with a ratio proportional to the sumScores.\\n84: func calculatePriority(sumScores int64, numContainers int) int64 {\\n85: \\tmaxThreshold := maxContainerThreshold * int64(numContainers)\\n86: \\tif sumScores < minThreshold {\\n87: \\t\\tsumScores = minThreshold\\n88: \\t} else if sumScores > maxThreshold {\\n89: \\t\\tsumScores = maxThreshold\\n90: \\t}\\n91: \\n92: \\treturn framework.MaxNodeScore * (sumScores - minThreshold) / (maxThreshold - minThreshold)\\n93: }\\n94: \\n95: // sumImageScores returns the sum of image scores of all the containers that are already on the node.\\n96: // Each image receives a raw score of its size, scaled by scaledImageScore. The raw scores are later used to calculate\\n97: // the final score.\\n98: func sumImageScores(nodeInfo *framework.NodeInfo, pod *v1.Pod, totalNumNodes int) int64 {\\n99: \\tvar sum int64\\n100: \\tfor _, container := range pod.Spec.InitContainers {\\n101: \\t\\tif state, ok := nodeInfo.ImageStates[normalizedImageName(container.Image)]; ok {\\n102: \\t\\t\\tsum += scaledImageScore(state, totalNumNodes)\\n103: \\t\\t}\\n104: \\t}\\n105: \\tfor _, container := range pod.Spec.Containers {\\n106: \\t\\tif state, ok := nodeInfo.ImageStates[normalizedImageName(container.Image)]; ok {\\n107: \\t\\t\\tsum += scaledImageScore(state, totalNumNodes)\\n108: \\t\\t}\\n109: \\t}\\n110: \\treturn sum\\n111: }\\n112: \\n113: // scaledImageScore returns an adaptively scaled score for the given state of an image.\\n114: // The size of the image is used as the base score, scaled by a factor which considers how much nodes the image has \\\"spread\\\" to.\\n115: // This heuristic aims to mitigate the undesirable \\\"node heating problem\\\", i.e., pods get assigned to the same or\\n116: // a few nodes due to image locality.\\n117: func scaledImageScore(imageState *framework.ImageStateSummary, totalNumNodes int) int64 {\\n118: \\tspread := float64(imageState.NumNodes) / float64(totalNumNodes)\\n119: \\treturn int64(float64(imageState.Size) * spread)\\n120: }\\n121: \\n122: // normalizedImageName returns the CRI compliant name for a given image.\\n123: // TODO: cover the corner cases of missed matches, e.g,\\n124: // 1. Using Docker as runtime and docker.io/library/test:tag in pod spec, but only test:tag will present in node status\\n125: // 2. Using the implicit registry, i.e., test:tag or library/test:tag in pod spec but only docker.io/library/test:tag\\n126: // in node status; note that if users consistently use one registry format, this should not happen.\\n127: func normalizedImageName(name string) string {\\n128: \\tif strings.LastIndex(name, \\\":\\\") <= strings.LastIndex(name, \\\"/\\\") {\\n129: \\t\\tname = name + \\\":latest\\\"\\n130: \\t}\\n131: \\treturn name\\n132: }\\n133: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0@917ed05e89979783903c4ca4563f0fc4120df4e2/-/blob/pkg/scheduler/framework/plugins/imagelocality/image_locality.go\"}"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01XWJuUhcLz3YAPP4ZJhVxtm","session_id":"4e692961-63fc-432c-82cc-07b297ef7c6b","uuid":"18116b5b-9a15-48a8-b0d5-9c0623e57c79"}

NodeAffinity is a plugin that checks if a pod node selector matches the node label.\ntype NodeAffinity struct {\n\thandle              framework.Handle\n\taddedNodeSelector   *nodeaffinity.NodeSelector\n\taddedPrefSchedTerms *nodeaffinity.PreferredSchedulingTerms\n}\n\nvar _ framework.PreFilterPlugin = &NodeAffinity{}\nvar _ framework.FilterPlugin = &NodeAffinity{}\nvar _ framework.PreScorePlugin = &NodeAffinity{}\nvar _ framework.ScorePlugin = &NodeAffinity{}\nvar _ framework.EnqueueExtensions = &NodeAffinity{}\n\nconst (\n\t// Name is the name of the plugin used in the plugin registry and configurations.\n\tName = names.NodeAffinity\n\n\t// preScoreStateKey is the key in CycleState to NodeAffinity pre-computed data for Scoring.\n\tpreScoreStateKey = \"PreScore\" + Name\n\n\t// preFilterStateKey is the key in CycleState to NodeAffinity pre-compute data for Filtering.\n\tpreFilterStateKey = \"PreFilter\" + Name\n\n\t// ErrReasonPod is the reason for Pod's node affinity/selector not matching.\n\tErrReasonPod = \"node(s) didn't match Pod's node affinity/selector\"\n\n\t// errReasonEnforced is the reason for added node affinity not matching.\n\terrReasonEnforced = \"node(s) didn't match scheduler-enforced node affinity\"\n\n\t// errReasonConflict is the reason for pod's conflicting affinity rules.\n\terrReasonConflict = \"pod affinity terms conflict\"\n)\n\n// Name returns name of the plugin. It is used in logs, etc.\nfunc (pl *NodeAffinity) Name() string {\n\treturn Name\n}\n\ntype preFilterState struct {\n\trequiredNodeSelectorAndAffinity nodeaffinity.RequiredNodeAffinity\n}\n\n// Clone just returns the same state because it is not affected by pod additions or deletions.\nfunc (s *preFilterState) Clone() framework.StateData {\n\treturn s\n}\n\n// EventsToRegister returns the possible events that may make a Pod\n// failed by this plugin schedulable.\nfunc (pl *NodeAffinity) EventsToRegister() []framework.ClusterEventWithHint {\n\treturn []framework.ClusterEventWithHint{\n\t\t{Event: framework.ClusterEvent{Resource: framework.Node, ActionType: framework.Add | framework.Update}, QueueingHintFn: pl.isSchedulableAfterNodeChange},\n\t}\n}\n\n// isSchedulableAfterNodeChange is invoked whenever a node changed. It checks whether\n// that change made a previously unschedulable pod schedulable.\nfunc (pl *NodeAffinity) isSchedulableAfterNodeChange(logger klog.Logger, pod *v1.Pod, oldObj, newObj interface{}) (framework.QueueingHint, error) {\n\t_, modifiedNode, err := util.As[*v1.Node](oldObj, newObj)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn framework.Queue, err\n\t}\n\n\tif pl.addedNodeSelector != nil && !pl.addedNodeSelector.Match(modifiedNode) {\n\t\tlogger.V(4).Info(\"added or modified node didn't match scheduler-enforced node affinity and this event won't make the Pod schedulable\", \"pod\", klog.KObj(pod), \"node\", klog.KObj(modifiedNode))\n\t\treturn framework.QueueSkip, nil\n\t}\n\n\trequiredNodeAffinity := nodeaffinity.GetRequiredNodeAffinity(pod)\n\tisMatched, err := requiredNodeAffinity.Match(modifiedNode)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn framework.Queue, err\n\t}\n\tif isMatched {\n\t\tlogger.V(4).Info(\"node was created or updated, and matches with the pod's NodeAffinity\", \"pod\", klog.KObj(pod), \"node\", klog.KObj(modifiedNode))\n\t\treturn framework.Queue, nil\n\t}\n\n\t// TODO: also check if the original node meets the pod's requestments once preCheck is completely removed.\n\t// See: https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/issues/110175\n\n\tlogger.V(4).Info(\"node was created or updated, but it doesn't make this pod schedulable\", \"pod\", klog.KObj(pod), \"node\", klog.KObj(modifiedNode))\n\treturn framework.QueueSkip, nil\n}\n\n// PreFilter builds and writes cycle state used by Filter.\nfunc (pl *NodeAffinity) PreFilter(ctx context.Context, cycleState *framework.CycleState, pod *v1.Pod) (*framework.PreFilterResult, *framework.Status) {\n\taffinity := pod.Spec.Affinity\n\tnoNodeAffinity := (affinity == nil ||\n\t\taffinity.NodeAffinity == nil ||\n\t\taffinity.NodeAffinity.RequiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution == nil)\n\tif noNodeAffinity && pl.addedNodeSelector == nil && pod.Spec.NodeSelector == nil {\n\t\t// NodeAffinity Filter has nothing to do with the Pod.\n\t\treturn nil, framework.NewStatus(framework.Skip)\n\t}\n\n\tstate := &preFilterState{requiredNodeSelectorAndAffinity: nodeaffinity.GetRequiredNodeAffinity(pod)}\n\tcycleState.Write(preFilterStateKey, state)\n\n\tif noNodeAffinity || len(affinity.NodeAffinity.RequiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution.NodeSelectorTerms) == 0 {\n\t\treturn nil, nil\n\t}\n\n\t// Check if there is affinity to a specific node and return it.\n\tterms := affinity.NodeAffinity.RequiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution.NodeSelectorTerms\n\tvar nodeNames sets.Set[string]\n\tfor _, t := range terms {\n\t\tvar termNodeNames sets.Set[string]\n\t\tfor _, r := range t.MatchFields {\n\t\t\tif r.Key == metav1.ObjectNameField && r.Operator == v1.NodeSelectorOpIn {\n\t\t\t\t// The requirements represent ANDed constraints, and so we need to\n\t\t\t\t// find the intersection of nodes.\n\t\t\t\ts := sets.New(r.Values...)\n\t\t\t\tif termNodeNames == nil {\n\t\t\t\t\ttermNodeNames = s\n\t\t\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t\t\ttermNodeNames = termNodeNames.Intersection(s)\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t\tif termNodeNames == nil {\n\t\t\t// If this term has no node.Name field affinity,\n\t\t\t// then all nodes are eligible because the terms are ORed.\n\t\t\treturn nil, nil\n\t\t}\n\t\tnodeNames = nodeNames.Union(termNodeNames)\n\t}\n\t// If nodeNames is not nil, but length is 0, it means each term have conflicting affinity to node.Name;\n\t// therefore, pod will not match any node.\n\tif nodeNames != nil && len(nodeNames) == 0 {\n\t\treturn nil, framework.NewStatus(framework.UnschedulableAndUnresolvable, errReasonConflict)\n\t} else if len(nodeNames) > 0 {\n\t\treturn &framework.PreFilterResult{NodeNames: nodeNames}, nil\n\t}\n\treturn nil, nil\n\n}\n\n// PreFilterExtensions not necessary for this plugin as state doesn't depend on pod additions or deletions.\nfunc (pl *NodeAffinity) PreFilterExtensions() framework.PreFilterExtensions {\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// Filter checks if the Node matches the Pod .spec.affinity.nodeAffinity and\n// the plugin's added affinity.\nfunc (pl *NodeAffinity) Filter(ctx context.Context, state *framework.CycleState, pod *v1.Pod, nodeInfo *framework.NodeInfo) *framework.Status {\n\tnode := nodeInfo.Node()\n\n\tif pl.addedNodeSelector != nil && !pl.addedNodeSelector.Match(node) {\n\t\treturn framework.NewStatus(framework.UnschedulableAndUnresolvable, errReasonEnforced)\n\t}\n\n\ts, err := getPreFilterState(state)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\t// Fallback to calculate requiredNodeSelector and requiredNodeAffinity\n\t\t// here when PreFilter is They correspond to a reasonable size range for\n// container images compressed and stored in registries; 90%ile of images on dockerhub drops into this range.\nconst (\n\tmb                    int64 = 1024 * 1024\n\tminThreshold          int64 = 23 * mb\n\tmaxContainerThreshold int64 = 1000 * mb\n)\n\n// ImageLocality is a score plugin that favors nodes that already have requested pod container's images.\ntype ImageLocality struct {\n\thandle framework.Handle\n}\n\nvar _ framework.ScorePlugin = &ImageLocality{}\n\n// Name is the name of the plugin used in the plugin registry and configurations.\nconst Name = names.ImageLocality\n\n// Name returns name of the plugin. It is used in logs, etc.\nfunc (pl *ImageLocality) Name() string {\n\treturn Name\n}\n\n// Score invoked at the score extension point.\nfunc (pl *ImageLocality) Score(ctx context.Context, state *framework.CycleState, pod *v1.Pod, nodeName string) (int64, *framework.Status) {\n\tnodeInfo, err := pl.handle.SnapshotSharedLister().NodeInfos().Get(nodeName)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn 0, framework.AsStatus(fmt.Errorf(\"getting node %q from Snapshot: %w\", nodeName, err))\n\t}\n\n\tnodeInfos, err := pl.handle.SnapshotSharedLister().NodeInfos().List()\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn 0, framework.AsStatus(err)\n\t}\n\ttotalNumNodes := len(nodeInfos)\n\n\timageScores := sumImageScores(nodeInfo, pod, totalNumNodes)\n\tscore := calculatePriority(imageScores, len(pod.Spec.InitContainers)+len(pod.Spec.Containers))\n\n\treturn score, nil\n}\n\n// ScoreNormalizer of the Score plugin.\nfunc (pl *ImageLocality) ScoreNormalizer() framework.ScoreNormalizer {\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// New initializes a new plugin and returns it.\nfunc New(_ context.Context, _ runtime.Object, h framework.Handle) (framework.Plugin, error) {\n\treturn &ImageLocality{handle: h}, nil\n}\n\n// calculatePriority returns the priority of a node. Given the sumScores of requested images on the node, the node's\n// priority is obtained by scaling the maximum priority value with a ratio proportional to the sumScores.\nfunc calculatePriority(sumScores int64, numContainers int) int64 {\n\tmaxThreshold := maxContainerThreshold * int64(numContainers)\n\tif sumScores < minThreshold {\n\t\tsumScores = minThreshold\n\t} else if sumScores > maxThreshold {\n\t\tsumScores = maxThreshold\n\t}\n\n\treturn framework.MaxNodeScore * (sumScores - minThreshold) / (maxThreshold - minThreshold)\n}\n\n// sumImageScores returns the sum of image scores of all the containers that are already on the node.\n// Each image receives a raw score of its size, scaled by scaledImageScore. The raw scores are later used to calculate\n// the final score.\nfunc sumImageScores(nodeInfo *framework.NodeInfo, pod *v1.Pod, totalNumNodes int) int64 {\n\tvar sum int64\n\tfor _, container := range pod.Spec.InitContainers {\n\t\tif state, ok := nodeInfo.ImageStates[normalizedImageName(container.Image)]; ok {\n\t\t\tsum += scaledImageScore(state, totalNumNodes)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\tfor _, container := range pod.Spec.Containers {\n\t\tif state, ok := nodeInfo.ImageStates[normalizedImageName(container.Image)]; ok {\n\t\t\tsum += scaledImageScore(state, totalNumNodes)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn sum\n}\n\n// scaledImageScore returns an adaptively scaled score for the given state of an image.\n// The size of the image is used as the base score, scaled by a factor which considers how much nodes the image has \"spread\" to.\n// This heuristic aims to mitigate the undesirable \"node heating problem\", i.e., pods get assigned to the same or\n// a few nodes due to image locality.\nfunc scaledImageScore(imageState *framework.ImageStateSummary, totalNumNodes int) int64 {\n\tspread := float64(imageState.NumNodes) / float64(totalNumNodes)\n\treturn int64(float64(imageState.Size) * spread)\n}\n\n// normalizedImageName returns the CRI compliant name for a given image.\n// TODO: cover the corner cases of missed matches, e.g,\n// 1. Using Docker as runtime and docker.io/library/test:tag in pod spec, but only test:tag will present in node status\n// 2. Using the implicit registry, i.e., test:tag or library/test:tag in pod spec but only docker.io/library/test:tag\n// in node status; note that if users consistently use one registry format, this should not happen.\nfunc normalizedImageName(name string) string {\n\tif strings.LastIndex(name, \":\") <= strings.LastIndex(name, \"/\") {\n\t\tname = name + \":latest\"\n\t}\n\treturn name\n}\n"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":4,"cache_creation_input_tokens":4384,"cache_read_input_tokens":112335,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":4384},"output_tokens":4,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01XWJuUhcLz3YAPP4ZJhVxtm","session_id":"4e692961-63fc-432c-82cc-07b297ef7c6b","uuid":"6733e0e0-081b-48a0-be85-6e0931341e67"}

{\n\treturn f.Ignorable\n}\n\n// SupportsPreemption returns true indicating the extender supports preemption.\nfunc (f *FakeExtender) SupportsPreemption() bool {\n\t// Assume preempt verb is always defined.\n\treturn true\n}\n\n// ProcessPreemption implements the extender preempt function.\nfunc (f *FakeExtender) ProcessPreemption(\n\tpod *v1.Pod,\n\tnodeNameToVictims map[string]*extenderv1.Victims,\n\tnodeInfos framework.NodeInfoLister,\n) (map[string]*extenderv1.Victims, error) {\n\tnodeNameToVictimsCopy := map[string]*extenderv1.Victims{}\n\t// We don't want to change the original nodeNameToVictims\n\tfor k, v := range nodeNameToVictims {\n\t\t// In real world implementation, extender's user should have their own way to get node object\n\t\t// by name if needed (e.g. query kube-apiserver etc).\n\t\t//\n\t\t// For test purpose, we just use node from parameters directly.\n\t\tnodeNameToVictimsCopy[k] = v\n\t}\n\n\t// If Extender.ProcessPreemption ever gets extended with a context parameter, then the logger should be retrieved from that.\n\t// Now, in order not to modify the Extender interface, we get the logger from klog.TODO()\n\tlogger := klog.TODO()\n\tfor nodeName, victims := range nodeNameToVictimsCopy {\n\t\t// Try to do preemption on extender side.\n\t\tnodeInfo, _ := nodeInfos.Get(nodeName)\n\t\textenderVictimPods, extenderPDBViolations, fits, err := f.selectVictimsOnNodeByExtender(logger, pod, nodeInfo)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn nil, err\n\t\t}\n\t\t// If it's unfit after extender's preemption, this node is unresolvable by preemption overall,\n\t\t// let's remove it from potential preemption nodes.\n\t\tif !fits {\n\t\t\tdelete(nodeNameToVictimsCopy, nodeName)\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t// Append new victims to original victims\n\t\t\tnodeNameToVictimsCopy[nodeName].Pods = append(victims.Pods, extenderVictimPods...)\n\t\t\tnodeNameToVictimsCopy[nodeName].NumPDBViolations = victims.NumPDBViolations + int64(extenderPDBViolations)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn nodeNameToVictimsCopy, nil\n}\n\n// selectVictimsOnNodeByExtender checks the given nodes->pods map with predicates on extender's side.\n// Returns:\n// 1. More victim pods (if any) amended by preemption phase of extender.\n// 2. Number of violating victim (used to calculate PDB).\n// 3. Fits or not after preemption phase on extender's side.\nfunc (f *FakeExtender) selectVictimsOnNodeByExtender(logger klog.Logger, pod *v1.Pod, node *framework.NodeInfo) ([]*v1.Pod, int, bool, error) {\n\t// If a extender support preemption but have no cached node info, let's run filter to make sure\n\t// default scheduler's decision still stand with given pod and node.\n\tif !f.NodeCacheCapable {\n\t\terr := f.runPredicate(pod, node)\n\t\tif err.IsSuccess() {\n\t\t\treturn []*v1.Pod{}, 0, true, nil\n\t\t} else if err.IsRejected() {\n\t\t\treturn nil, 0, false, nil\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\treturn nil, 0, false, err.AsError()\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Otherwise, as a extender support preemption and have cached node info, we will assume cachedNodeNameToInfo is available\n\t// and get cached node info by given node name.\n\tnodeInfoCopy := f.CachedNodeNameToInfo[node.Node().Name].Snapshot()\n\n\tvar potentialVictims []*v1.Pod\n\n\tremovePod := func(rp *v1.Pod) error {\n\t\treturn nodeInfoCopy.RemovePod(logger, rp)\n\t}\n\taddPod := func(ap *v1.Pod) {\n\t\tnodeInfoCopy.AddPod(ap)\n\t}\n\t// As the first step, remove all the lower priority pods from the node and\n\t// check if the given pod can be scheduled.\n\tpodPriority := corev1helpers.PodPriority(pod)\n\tfor _, p := range nodeInfoCopy.Pods {\n\t\tif corev1helpers.PodPriority(p.Pod) < podPriority {\n\t\t\tpotentialVictims = append(potentialVictims, p.Pod)\n\t\t\tif err := removePod(p.Pod); err != nil {\n\t\t\t\treturn nil, 0, false, err\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\tsort.Slice(potentialVictims, func(i, j int) bool { return util.MoreImportantPod(potentialVictims[i], potentialVictims[j]) })\n\n\t// If the new pod does not fit after removing all the lower priority pods,\n\t// we are almost done and this node is not suitable for preemption.\n\tstatus := f.runPredicate(pod, nodeInfoCopy)\n\tif status.IsSuccess() {\n\t\t// pass\n\t} else if status.IsRejected() {\n\t\t// does not fit\n\t\treturn nil, 0, false, nil\n\t} else {\n\t\t// internal errors\n\t\treturn nil, 0, false, status.AsError()\n\t}\n\n\tvar victims []*v1.Pod\n\n\t// TODO(harry): handle PDBs in the future.\n\tnumViolatingVictim := 0\n\n\treprievePod := func(p *v1.Pod) bool {\n\t\taddPod(p)\n\t\tstatus := f.runPredicate(pod, nodeInfoCopy)\n\t\tif !status.IsSuccess() {\n\t\t\tif err := removePod(p); err != nil {\n\t\t\t\treturn false\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tvictims = append(victims, p)\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn status.IsSuccess()\n\t}\n\n\t// For now, assume all potential victims to be non-violating.\n\t// Now we try to reprieve non-violating victims.\n\tfor _, p := range potentialVictims {\n\t\treprievePod(p)\n\t}\n\n\treturn victims, numViolatingVictim, true, nil\n}\n\n// runPredicate run predicates of extender one by one for given pod and node.\n// Returns: fits or not.\nfunc (f *FakeExtender) runPredicate(pod *v1.Pod, node *framework.NodeInfo) *framework.Status {\n\tfor _, predicate := range f.Predicates {\n\t\tstatus := predicate(pod, node)\n\t\tif !status.IsSuccess() {\n\t\t\treturn status\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn framework.NewStatus(framework.Success)\n}\n\n// Filter implements the extender Filter function.\nfunc (f *FakeExtender) Filter(pod *v1.Pod, nodes []*framework.NodeInfo) ([]*framework.NodeInfo, extenderv1.FailedNodesMap, extenderv1.FailedNodesMap, error) {\n\tvar filtered []*framework.NodeInfo\n\tfailedNodesMap := extenderv1.FailedNodesMap{}\n\tfailedAndUnresolvableMap := extenderv1.FailedNodesMap{}\n\tfor _, node := range nodes {\n\t\tstatus := f.runPredicate(pod, node)\n\t\tif status.IsSuccess() {\n\t\t\tfiltered = append(filtered, node)\n\t\t} else if status.Code() == framework.Unschedulable {\n\t\t\tfailedNodesMap[node.Node().Name] = f.NodeCacheCapable {\n\t\treturn filtered, failedNodesMap, failedAndUnresolvableMap, nil\n\t}\n\treturn filtered, failedNodesMap, failedAndUnresolvableMap, nil\n}\n\n// Prioritize implements the extenderv1.HostPriorityList{}\n\tcombinedScores := map[string]int64{}\n\tfor _, prioritizer := range f.Prioritizers {\n\t\tweight := prioritizer.Weight\n\t\tif weight == 0 {\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\t\tpriorityFunc := prioritizer.Function\n\t\tprioritizedList, err := priorityFunc(pod, nodes)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn &extenderv1.HostPriorityList{}, 0, err\n\t\t}\n\t\tfor _, hostEntry := range *prioritizedList {\n\t\t\tcombinedScores[hostEntry.Name] += hostEntry.Score * weight\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\tfor host, score := range combinedScores {\n\t\tresult = \\t\\\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/scheduler/framework\\\"\\n29: )\\n30: \\n31: const preScoreStateKey = \\\"PreScore\\\" + Name\\n32: const invalidScore = -1\\n33: \\n34: // preScoreState computed at matching pods.\\n41: \\tTopologyPairToPodCounts map[topologyPair]*int64\\n42: \\t// TopologyNormalizingWeight is the weight we give to the counts per topology.\\n43: \\t// This allows the pod counts of smaller topologies to not be watered down by\\n44: \\t// bigger ones.\\n45: \\tTopologyNormalizingWeight []float64\\n46: }\\n47: \\n48: // Clone implements the mandatory Clone interface. We don't really copy the data since\\n49: // there is no need for that.\\n50: func (s *preScoreState) Clone() framework.StateData {\\n51: \\treturn s\\n52: }\\n53: \\n54: // initPreScoreState iterates \\\"filteredNodes\\\" to filter out the nodes which\\n55: // don't have required topologyKey(s), and initialize:\\n56: // 1) s.TopologyPairToPodCounts: keyed with both eligible topology pair and node names.\\n57: // 2) s.IgnoredNodes: the set of nodes that shouldn't be scored.\\n58: // 3) s.TopologyNormalizingWeight: The weight to be given to each constraint based on the number of values in a topology.\\n59: func (pl *PodTopologySpread) initPreScoreState(s *preScoreState, pod *v1.Pod, filteredNodes []*framework.NodeInfo, requireAllTopologies bool) error {\\n60: \\tvar err error\\n61: \\tif len(pod.Spec.TopologySpreadConstraints) > 0 {\\n62: \\t\\ts.Constraints, err = pl.filterTopologySpreadConstraints(\\n63: \\t\\t\\tpod.Spec.TopologySpreadConstraints,\\n64: \\t\\t\\tpod.Labels,\\n65: \\t\\t\\tv1.ScheduleAnyway,\\n66: \\t\\t)\\n67: \\t\\tif err != nil {\\n68: \\t\\t\\treturn fmt.Errorf(\\\"obtaining pod's soft topology spread constraints: %w\\\", err)\\n69: \\t\\t}\\n70: \\t} else {\\n71: \\t\\ts.Constraints, err = pl.buildDefaultConstraints(pod, v1.ScheduleAnyway)\\n72: \\t\\tif err != nil {\\n73: \\t\\t\\treturn fmt.Errorf(\\\"setting default soft topology spread constraints: %w\\\", err)\\n74: \\t\\t}\\n75: \\t}\\n76: \\tif len(s.Constraints) == 0 {\\n77: \\t\\treturn nil\\n78: \\t}\\n79: \\ttopoSize := make([]int, len(s.Constraints))\\n80: \\tfor _, node := range filteredNodes {\\n81: \\t\\tif requireAllTopologies && !nodeLabelsMatchSpreadConstraints(node.Node().Labels, s.Constraints) {\\n82: \\t\\t\\t// Nodes which don't have all required topologyKeys present are ignored\\n83: \\t\\t\\t// when scoring later.\\n84: \\t\\t\\ts.IgnoredNodes.Insert(node.Node().Name)\\n85: \\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n86: \\t\\t}\\n87: \\t\\tfor i, constraint := range s.Constraints {\\n88: \\t\\t\\t// per-node counts are calculated during Score.\\n89: \\t\\t\\tif constraint.TopologyKey == v1.LabelHostname {\\n90: \\t\\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n91: \\t\\t\\t}\\n92: \\t\\t\\tpair := topologyPair{key: constraint.TopologyKey, value: node.Node().Labels[constraint.TopologyKey]}\\n93: \\t\\t\\tif s.TopologyPairToPodCounts[pair] == nil {\\n94: \\t\\t\\t\\ts.TopologyPairToPodCounts[pair] = new(int64)\\n95: \\t\\t\\t\\ttopoSize[i]++\\n96: \\t\\t\\t}\\n97: \\t\\t}\\n98: \\t}\\n99: \\n100: \\ts.TopologyNormalizingWeight = make([]float64, nil\\n186: }\\n187: \\n188: // Score invoked at the Score extension point.\\n189: // The \\\"score\\\" returned in this function is the matching number of pods on the `nodeName`,\\n190: // it is normalized later.\\n191: func (pl *PodTopologySpread) Score(ctx context.Context, cycleState *framework.CycleState, pod *v1.Pod, nodeName string) (int64, *framework.Status) {\\n192: \\tnodeInfo, err := *framework.Status {\\n228: \\ts, err := getPreScoreState(cycleState)\\n229: \\tif err != nil {\\n230: \\t\\treturn framework.AsStatus(err)\\n231: \\t}\\n232: \\tif s == nil {\\n233: \\t\\treturn = framework.MaxNodeScore * (maxScore + minScore - s) / maxScore\\n264: \\t}\\n265: \\treturn nil\\n266: }\\n267: \\n268: // ScoreExtensions of the Score plugin.\\n269: func (pl *PodTopologySpread) \\n286: // topologyNormalizingWeight calculates the weight for the topology, based on\\n287: // the number of values that exist for a topology.\\n288: // Since <size> is at least 1 (all nodes that passed the Filters are in the\\n289: // same topology), and k8s supports 5k nodes, the result is in the interval\\n290: // <1.09, 8.52>.\\n291: //\\n292: // Note: <size> could also be zero when no nodes have the required topologies,\\n293: // however we don't care about topology weight in this case as we return a 0\\n294: // score for all nodes.\\n295: func topologyNormalizingWeight(size int) maxSkew and the topology weight.\\n301: // `maxSkew-1` is added to the score so that differences between topology\\n302: // domains get watered down, controlling the tolerance of the score to skews.\\n303: func scoreForCount(cnt int64, maxSkew int32, tpWeight float64) float64 {\\n304: \\treturn float64(cnt)*tpWeight + float64(maxSkew-1)\\n305: }\\n306: interpodaffinity.preScoreState error\\\", c)\\n235: \\t}\\n236: \\treturn s, nil\\n237: }\\n238: \\n239: // Score invoked at the Score extension point.\\n240: // The \\\"score\\\" returned in this function is the sum of weights got from cycleState which have its topologyKey matching with the node's labels.\\n241: // it is normalized later.\\n242: // Note: the returned \\\"score\\\" is positive for pod-affinity, and negative for pod-antiaffinity.\\n243: func (pl *InterPodAffinity) Score(ctx context.Context, cycleState *framework.CycleState, pod *v1.Pod, nodeName string) (int64, *framework.Status) {\\n244: \\tnodeInfo, err := pl.sharedLister.NodeInfos().Get(nodeName)\\n245: \\tif err != nil {\\n246: \\t\\treturn 0, framework.AsStatus(fmt.Errorf(\\\"failed to get node %q from framework.AsStatus(err)\\n253: \\t}\\n254: \\tvar score int64\\n255: \\tfor tpKey, tpValues := range s.topologyScore {\\n256: \\t\\tif v, exist := node.Labels[tpKey]; exist {\\n257: \\t\\t\\tscore += NormalizeScore(ctx context.Context, cycleState *framework.CycleState, pod *v1.Pod, scores framework.NodeScoreList) *framework.Status {\\n266: \\ts, err := getPreScoreState(cycleState)\\n267: \\tif err balancedResourceScorer(requested, allocable []int64) int64 {\\n139: \\tvar resourceToFractions []float64\\n140: \\tvar totalFraction float64\\n141: \\tfor i := range requested {\\n142: \\t\\tif allocable[i] == 0 {\\n143: \\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n144: \\t\\t}\\n145: \\t\\tfraction := float64(requested[i]) / float64(allocable[i])\\n146: \\t\\tif fraction > 1 {\\n147: \\t\\t\\tfraction = 1\\n148: \\t\\t}\\n149: \\t\\ttotalFraction += fraction\\n150: \\t\\tresourceToFractions = append(resourceToFractions, fraction)\\n151: \\t}\\n152: \\n153: \\tstd := 0.0\\n154: \\n155: \\t// For most cases, resources are limited to cpu and memory, the std could be simplified to std := (fraction1-fraction2)/2\\n156: \\t// len(fractions) > 2: calculate std based on the well-known formula - root square of Σ((fraction(i)-mean)^2)/len(fractions)\\n157: \\t// Otherwise, set the std to zero is enough.\\n158: \\tif len(resourceToFractions) == 2 {\\n159: \\t\\tstd = math.Abs((resourceToFractions[0] - resourceToFractions[1]) / 2)\\n160: \\n161: \\t} else if len(resourceToFractions) > 2 {\\n162: \\t\\tmean := totalFraction / float64(len(resourceToFractions))\\n163: \\t\\tvar sum float64\\n164: \\t\\tfor _, fraction := range resourceToFractions {\\n165: \\t\\t\\tsum = sum + (fraction-mean)*(fraction-mean)\\n166: \\t\\t}\\n167: \\t\\tstd = math.Sqrt(sum / float64(len(resourceToFractions)))\\n168: \\t}\\n169: \\n170: \\t// STD (standard deviation) is always a positive value. 1-deviation lets the score to be higher for node which has least deviation and\\n171: \\t// multiplying it with `MaxNodeScore` provides the scaling factor needed.\\n172: \\treturn int64((1 - std) * float64(framework.MaxNodeScore))\\n173: }\\n174: In contrast, we sum the resource vectors for\\n183: // regular containers since they run simultaneously.\\n184: //\\n185: // # The resources defined for Overhead should be added to the calculated Resource request sum\\n186: //\\n187: // Example:\\n188: //\\n189: // Pod:\\n190: //\\n191: //\\tInitContainers\\n192: //\\t  IC1:\\n193: //\\t    CPU: 2\\n194: //\\t    Memory: 1G\\n195: //\\t  IC2:\\n196: //\\t    CPU: 2\\n197: //\\t    Memory: 3G\\n198: //\\tContainers\\n199: //\\t  C1:\\n200: //\\t    CPU: 2\\n201: //\\t    Memory: 1G\\n202: //\\t  C2:\\n203: //\\t    CPU: 1\\n204: //\\t    Memory: 1G\\n205: //\\n206: // Result: CPU: 3, Memory: 3G\\n207: func computePodResourceRequest(pod *v1.Pod) *preFilterState {\\n208: \\t// pod hasn't scheduled yet so we don't need to worry about InPlacePodVerticalScalingEnabled\\n209: \\treqs := resource.PodRequests(pod, resource.PodResourcesOptions{})\\n210: \\tresult := &preFilterState{}\\n211: \\tresult.SetMaxResource(reqs)\\n212: \\treturn result\\n213: }\\n214: \\n215: // PreFilter invoked at the prefilter extension point.\\n216: func (f *Fit) PreFilter(ctx context.Context, cycleState *framework.CycleState, pod *v1.Pod) (*framework.PreFilterResult, *framework.Status) {\\n217: \\tif !f.enableSidecarContainers && hasRestartableInitContainer(pod) {\\n218: \\t\\t// Scheduler will calculate resources usage for a Pod containing\\n219: \\t\\t// restartable init containers that will be equal or more than kubelet will\\n220: \\t\\t// require to run the Pod. updated. It checks whether\\n264: // that change made a previously unschedulable pod schedulable.\\n265: func (f *Fit) isSchedulableAfterPodChange(logger klog.Logger, pod *v1.Pod, oldObj, newObj interface{}) (framework.QueueingHint, error) {\\n266: \\toriginalPod, modifiedPod, err := schedutil.As[*v1.Pod](oldObj, newObj)\\n267: \\tif err != nil {\\n268: \\t\\treturn framework.Queue, err\\n269: \\t}\\n270: \\n271: \\tif modifiedPod == nil {\\n272: \\t\\tif originalPod.Spec.NodeName == \\\"\\\" {\\n273: \\t\\t\\tlogger.V(5).Info(\\\"the deleted pod was unscheduled and it wouldn't make the unscheduled pod schedulable\\\", \\\"pod\\\", klog.KObj(pod), \\\"deletedPod\\\", klog.KObj(originalPod))\\n274: \\t\\t\\treturn framework.QueueSkip, nil\\n275: \\t\\t}\\n276: \\t\\tlogger.V(5).Info(\\\"another scheduled pod was deleted, and it may make the unscheduled pod schedulable\\\", \\\"pod\\\", klog.KObj(pod), \\\"deletedPod\\\", klog.KObj(originalPod))\\n277: \\t\\treturn framework.Queue, nil\\n278: \\t}\\n279: \\n280: \\tif !f.enableInPlacePodVerticalScaling {\\n281: \\t\\t// If InPlacePodVerticalScaling (KEP 1287) is disabled, it cannot free up resources.\\n282: \\t\\tlogger.V(5).Info(\\\"another pod was modified, but InPlacePodVerticalScaling is disabled, so it doesn't make the unscheduled pod schedulable\\\", \\\"pod\\\", klog.KObj(pod), \\\"modifiedPod\\\", klog.KObj(modifiedPod))\\n283: \\t\\treturn framework.QueueSkip, nil\\n284: \\t}\\n285: \\n286: \\t// Modifications may or may not be relevant. \\t\\t{Event: framework.ClusterEvent{Resource: framework.StorageClass, ActionType: framework.Add | framework.Update}},\\n100: \\t\\t// We bind PVCs with PVs, so any changes may make the pods framework.ClusterEvent{Resource: framework.PersistentVolume, ActionType: framework.Add | framework.Update}},\\n103: \\t\\t// Pods may fail to find available PVs because the node labels do not\\n104: \\t\\t// match the storage class's allowed topologies or PV's node affinity.\\n105: \\t\\t// A new or updated node may make pods schedulable.\\n106: \\t\\t//\\n107: \\t\\t// A note about UpdateNodeTaint event:\\n108: \\t\\t// NodeAdd QueueingHint isn't always called because of the internal feature called preCheck.\\n109: \\t\\t// As a common problematic scenario,\\n110: \\t\\t// when a node is added but not ready, NodeAdd event is filtered out by preCheck and doesn't arrive.\\n111: \\t\\t// In such cases, this plugin may miss some events that actually make pods schedulable.\\n112: \\t\\t// As a workaround, we add UpdateNodeTaint event to catch the case.\\n113: \\t\\t// We can remove UpdateNodeTaint when we remove the preCheck feature.\\n114: \\t\\t// See: preScoreStateKey = \"PreScore\" + Name\nconst invalidScore = -1\n\n// preScoreState computed at PreScore and used at Score.\n// Fields are exported for comparison during testing.\ntype preScoreState struct {\n\tConstraints []topologySpreadConstraint\n\t// IgnoredNodes is a set of node names which miss some Constraints[*].topologyKey.\n\tIgnoredNodes sets.Set[string]\n\t// TopologyPairToPodCounts is keyed with topologyPair, and valued with the number of matching pods.\n\tTopologyPairToPodCounts map[topologyPair]*int64\n\t// TopologyNormalizingWeight is the weight we give to the counts per topology.\n\t// This allows the pod counts of smaller topologies to not be watered down by\n\t// bigger ones.\n\tTopologyNormalizingWeight []float64\n}\n\n// Clone implements the mandatory Clone interface. We don't really copy the data since\n// there is no need for that.\nfunc (s *preScoreState) Clone() framework.StateData {\n\treturn s\n}\n\n// initPreScoreState iterates \"filteredNodes\" to filter out the nodes which\n// don't have required topologyKey(s), and initialize:\n// 1) s.TopologyPairToPodCounts: keyed with both eligible topology pair and node names.\n// 2) s.IgnoredNodes: the set of nodes that shouldn't be scored.\n// 3) s.TopologyNormalizingWeight: The weight to be given to each constraint based on the number of values in a topology.\nfunc (pl *PodTopologySpread) initPreScoreState(s *preScoreState, pod *v1.Pod, filteredNodes []*framework.NodeInfo, requireAllTopologies bool) error {\n\tvar err error\n\tif len(pod.Spec.TopologySpreadConstraints) > 0 {\n\t\ts.Constraints, err = pl.filterTopologySpreadConstraints(\n\t\t\tpod.Spec.TopologySpreadConstraints,\n\t\t\tpod.Labels,\n\t\t\tv1.ScheduleAnyway,\n\t\t)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"obtaining pod's soft topology spread constraints: %w\", err)\n\t\t}\n\t} else {\n\t\ts.Constraints, err = pl.buildDefaultConstraints(pod, v1.ScheduleAnyway)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"setting default soft topology spread constraints: %w\", err)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\tif len(s.Constraints) == 0 {\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\ttopoSize := make([]int, len(s.Constraints))\n\tfor _, node := range filteredNodes {\n\t\tif requireAllTopologies && !nodeLabelsMatchSpreadConstraints(node.Node().Labels, s.Constraints) {\n\t\t\t// Nodes which don't have all required topologyKeys present are ignored\n\t\t\t// when scoring later.\n\t\t\ts.IgnoredNodes.Insert(node.Node().Name)\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\t\tfor i, constraint := range s.Constraints {\n\t\t\t// per-node counts are calculated during Score.\n\t\t\tif constraint.TopologyKey == v1.LabelHostname {\n\t\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tpair := topologyPair{key: constraint.TopologyKey, value: node.Node().Labels[constraint.TopologyKey]}\n\t\t\tif s.TopologyPairToPodCounts[pair] == nil {\n\t\t\t\ts.TopologyPairToPodCounts[pair] = new(int64)\n\t\t\t\ttopoSize[i]++\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\ts.TopologyNormalizingWeight = make([]float64, len(s.Constraints))\n\tfor i, c := range s.Constraints {\n\t\tsz := topoSize[i]\n\t\tif c.TopologyKey == v1.LabelHostname {\n\t\t\tsz = len(filteredNodes) - len(s.IgnoredNodes)\n\t\t}\n\t\ts.TopologyNormalizingWeight[i] = topologyNormalizingWeight(sz)\n\t}\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// PreScore builds and writes cycle state used by Score and NormalizeScore.\nfunc (pl *PodTopologySpread) PreScore(\n\tctx context.Context,\n\tcycleState *framework.CycleState,\n\tpod *v1.Pod,\n\tfilteredNodes []*framework.NodeInfo,\n) *framework.Status {\n\tallNodes, err := pl.sharedLister.NodeInfos().List()\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn framework.AsStatus(fmt.Errorf(\"getting all nodes: %w\", err))\n\t}\n\n\tif len(allNodes) == 0 {\n\t\t// No need to score.\n\t\treturn framework.NewStatus(framework.Skip)\n\t}\n\n\tstate := &preScoreState{\n\t\tIgnoredNodes:            sets.New[string](),\n\t\tTopologyPairToPodCounts: make(map[topologyPair]*int64),\n\t}\n\t// Only require that nodes have all the topology labels if using\n\t// non-system-default spreading rules. This allows nodes that don't have a\n\t// zone label to still have hostname spreading.\n\trequireAllTopologies := len(pod.Spec.TopologySpreadConstraints) > 0 || !pl.systemDefaulted\n\terr = pl.initPreScoreState(state, pod, filteredNodes, requireAllTopologies)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn framework.AsStatus(fmt.Errorf(\"calculating preScoreState: %w\", err))\n\t}\n\n\t// return Skip if incoming pod doesn't have soft topology spread Constraints.\n\tif len(state.Constraints) == 0 {\n\t\treturn framework.NewStatus(framework.Skip)\n\t}\n\n\t// Ignore parsing errors for backwards compatibility.\n\trequiredNodeAffinity := nodeaffinity.GetRequiredNodeAffinity(pod)\n\tprocessAllNode := func(i int) {\n\t\tnodeInfo := allNodes[i]\n\t\tnode := nodeInfo.Node()\n\n\t\tif !pl.enableNodeInclusionPolicyInPodTopologySpread {\n\t\t\t// `node` should satisfy incoming pod's NodeSelector/NodeAffinity\n\t\t\tif match, _ := requiredNodeAffinity.Match(node); !match {\n\t\t\t\treturn\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// All topologyKeys need to be present in `node`\n\t\tif requireAllTopologies && !nodeLabelsMatchSpreadConstraints(node.Labels, state.Constraints) {\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tfor _, c := range state.Constraints {\n\t\t\tif pl.enableNodeInclusionPolicyInPodTopologySpread &&\n\t\t\t\t!c.matchNodeInclusionPolicies(pod, node, requiredNodeAffinity) {\n\t\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tpair := topologyPair{key: c.TopologyKey, value: node.Labels[c.TopologyKey]}\n\t\t\t// If current topology pair is not associated with any candidate node,\n\t\t\t// continue to avoid unnecessary calculation.\n\t\t\t// Per-node counts are also skipped, as they are done during Score.\n\t\t\ttpCount := state.TopologyPairToPodCounts[pair]\n\t\t\tif tpCount == nil {\n\t\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tcount := countPodsMatchSelector(nodeInfo.Pods, c.Selector, pod.Namespace)\n\t\t\tatomic.AddInt64(tpCount, int64(count))\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\tpl.parallelizer.Until(ctx, len(allNodes), processAllNode, pl.Name())\n\n\tcycleState.Write(preScoreStateKey, state)\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// Score invoked at the Score extension point.\n// The \"score\" returned in this function is the matching number of pods on the `nodeName`,\n// it is normalized later.\nfunc (pl *PodTopologySpread) Score(ctx context.Context, cycleState *framework.CycleState, pod *v1.Pod, nodeName string) (int64, *framework.Status) {\n\tnodeInfo, err := pl.sharedLister.NodeInfos().Get(nodeName)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn 0, framework.AsStatus(fmt.Errorf(\"getting node %q from Snapshot: %w\", nodeName, err))\n\t}\n\n\tnode := nodeInfo.Node()\n\ts, err := getPreScoreState(cycleState)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn 0, framework.AsStatus(err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Return if the node is not qualified.\n\tif s.IgnoredNodes.Has(node.Name) {\n\t\treturn 0, nil\n\t}\n\n\t// For each present <pair>, current node gets a credit of <matchSum>.\n\t// And we sum up <matchSum> and return it as this node's score.\n\tvar score float64\n\tfor i, c := range s.Constraints {\n\t\tif tpVal, ok := node.Labels[c.TopologyKey]; ok {\n\t\t\tvar cnt int64\n\t\t\tif c.TopologyKey == v1.LabelHostname {\n\t\t\t\tcnt = int64(countPodsMatchSelector(nodeInfo.Pods, c.Selector, pod.Namespace))\n\t\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t\tpair := topologyPair{key: c.TopologyKey, value: tpVal}\n\t\t\t\tcnt = *s.TopologyPairToPodCounts[pair]\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tscore += scoreForCount(cnt, c.MaxSkew, s.TopologyNormalizingWeight[i])\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn int64(math.Round(score)), nil\n}\n\n// NormalizeScore invoked after scoring all nodes.\nfunc (pl *PodTopologySpread) NormalizeScore(ctx context.Context, cycleState *framework.CycleState, pod *v1.Pod, scores framework.NodeScoreList) *framework.Status {\n\ts, err := getPreScoreState(cycleState)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn framework.AsStatus(err)\n\t}\n\tif s == nil {\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\n\t// Calculate <minScore> and <maxScore>\n\tvar minScore int64 = math.MaxInt64\n\tvar maxScore int64\n\tfor i, score := range scores {\n\t\t// it's mandatory to check if <score.Name> is present in m.IgnoredNodes\n\t\tif s.IgnoredNodes.Has(score.Name) {\n\t\t\tscores[i].Score = invalidScore\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\t\tif score.Score < minScore {\n\t\t\tminScore = score.Score\n\t\t}\n\t\tif score.Score > maxScore {\n\t\t\tmaxScore = score.Score\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tfor i := range scores {\n\t\tif scores[i].Score == invalidScore {\n\t\t\tscores[i].Score = 0\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\t\tif maxScore == 0 {\n\t\t\tscores[i].Score = framework.MaxNodeScore\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\t\ts := scores[i].Score\n\t\tscores[i].Score = framework.MaxNodeScore * (maxScore + minScore - s) / maxScore\n\t}\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// ScoreNormalizer of the Score plugin.\nfunc (pl *PodTopologySpread) ScoreNormalizer() framework.ScoreNormalizer {\n\treturn pl\n}\n\nfunc getPreScoreState(cycleState *framework.CycleState) (*preScoreState, error) {\n\tc, err := cycleState.Read(preScoreStateKey)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"error reading %q from cycleState: %w\", preScoreStateKey, err)\n\t}\n\n\ts, ok := c.(*preScoreState)\n\tif !ok {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"%+v  convert to podtopologyspread.preScoreState error\", c)\n\t}\n\treturn s, nil\n}\n\n// topologyNormalizingWeight calculates the weight for the topology, based on\n// the number of values that exist for a topology.\n// Since <size> is at least 1 (all nodes that passed the Filters are in the\n// same topology), and k8s supports 5k nodes, the result is in the interval\n// <1.09, 8.52>.\n//\n// Note: <size> could also be zero when no nodes have the required topologies,\n// however we don't care about topology weight in this case as we return a 0\n// score for all nodes.\nfunc topologyNormalizingWeight(size int) float64 {\n\treturn math.Log(float64(size + 2))\n}\n\n// scoreForCount calculates the score based on number of matching pods in a\n// topology domain, the constraint's maxSkew and the topology weight.\n// `maxSkew-1` is added to the score so that differences between topology\n// domains get watered down, controlling the tolerance of the score to skews.\nfunc scoreForCount(cnt int64, maxSkew int32, tpWeight float64) float64 {\n\treturn float64(cnt)*tpWeight + float64(maxSkew-1)\n}\n"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":4,"cache_creation_input_tokens":549,"cache_read_input_tokens":68245,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":549},"output_tokens":1,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01USLfwmDaDcyrqUWgHcjXZn","session_id":"4e692961-63fc-432c-82cc-07b297ef7c6b","uuid":"a64ef389-7c44-497c-934b-256feb31ecb8"}

*framework.CycleState, pod *v1.Pod, scores framework.NodeScoreList) *framework.Status {\n\ts, err := getPreScoreState(cycleState)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn framework.AsStatus(err)\n\t}\n\tif maxCount {\n\t\t\tmaxCount = score\n\t\t}\n\t\tif score < minCount {\n\t\t\tminCount = score\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tmaxMinDiff := maxCount - minCount\n\tfor i := range scores {\n\t\tfScore := noderesources\n\nimport (\n\t\"context\"\n\t\"fmt\"\n\t\"math\"\n\n\tv1 \"k8s.io/api/core/v1\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime\"\n\t\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/scheduler/apis/config\"\n\t\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/scheduler/apis/config/validation\"\n\t\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/scheduler/framework\"\n\t\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/scheduler/framework/plugins/feature\"\n\t\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/scheduler/framework/plugins/names\"\n)\n\n// BalancedAllocation is a score plugin that calculates the difference between the cpu and memory fraction\n// of capacity, and prioritizes the host based on how close the two metrics are to each other.\ntype BalancedAllocation struct {\n\thandle framework.Handle\n\tresourceAllocationScorer\n}\n\nvar _ framework.PreScorePlugin = &BalancedAllocation{}\nvar _ framework.ScorePlugin = &BalancedAllocation{}\n\n// BalancedAllocationName is the name of the plugin used in the plugin registry and configurations.\nconst (\n\tBalancedAllocationName = names.NodeResourcesBalancedAllocation\n\n\t// balancedAllocationPreScoreStateKey is the key in CycleState to NodeResourcesBalancedAllocation pre-computed data for Scoring.\n\tbalancedAllocationPreScoreStateKey = \"PreScore\" + BalancedAllocationName\n)\n\n// balancedAllocationPreScoreState computed at PreScore and used at Score.\ntype balancedAllocationPreScoreState struct {\n\t// podRequests have the same order of the resources defined in NodeResourcesFitArgs.Resources,\n\t// same for other place we store a list like that.\n\tpodRequests []int64\n}\n\n// Clone implements the mandatory Clone interface. We don't really copy the data since\n// there is no need for that.\nfunc (s *balancedAllocationPreScoreState) Clone() framework.StateData {\n\treturn s\n}\n\n// PreScore calculates incoming pod's resource requests and writes them to the cycle state used.\nfunc (ba *BalancedAllocation) PreScore(ctx context.Context, cycleState *framework.CycleState, pod *v1.Pod, nodes []*framework.NodeInfo) *framework.Status {\n\tstate := &balancedAllocationPreScoreState{\n\t\tpodRequests: ba.calculatePodResourceRequestList(pod, ba.resources),\n\t}\n\tcycleState.Write(balancedAllocationPreScoreStateKey, state)\n\treturn nil\n}\n\nfunc getBalancedAllocationPreScoreState(cycleState *framework.CycleState) (*balancedAllocationPreScoreState, error) {\n\tc, err := cycleState.Read(balancedAllocationPreScoreStateKey)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"reading %q from cycleState: %w\", balancedAllocationPreScoreStateKey, err)\n\t}\n\n\ts, ok := c.(*balancedAllocationPreScoreState)\n\tif !ok {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"invalid PreScore state, got type %T\", c)\n\t}\n\treturn s, nil\n}\n\n// Name returns name of the plugin. It is used in logs, etc.\nfunc (ba *BalancedAllocation) Name() string {\n\treturn BalancedAllocationName\n}\n\n// Score invoked at the score extension point.\nfunc (ba *BalancedAllocation) Score(ctx context.Context, state *framework.CycleState, pod *v1.Pod, nodeName string) (int64, *framework.Status) {\n\tnodeInfo, err := ba.handle.SnapshotSharedLister().NodeInfos().Get(nodeName)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn 0, framework.AsStatus(fmt.Errorf(\"getting node %q from Snapshot: %w\", nodeName, err))\n\t}\n\n\ts, err := getBalancedAllocationPreScoreState(state)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\ts = &balancedAllocationPreScoreState{podRequests: ba.calculatePodResourceRequestList(pod, ba.resources)}\n\t}\n\n\t// ba.score favors nodes with balanced resource usage rate.\n\t// It calculates the standard deviation for those resources and prioritizes the node based on how close the usage of those resources is to each other.\n\t// Detail: score = (1 - std) * MaxNodeScore, where std is calculated by the root square of Σ((fraction(i)-mean)^2)/len(resources)\n\t// The algorithm is partly inspired by:\n\t// \"Wei Huang et al. An Energy Efficient Virtual Machine Placement Algorithm with Balanced Resource Utilization\"\n\treturn ba.score(ctx, pod, nodeInfo, s.podRequests)\n}\n\n// ScoreNormalizer of the Score plugin.\nfunc (ba *BalancedAllocation) ScoreNormalizer() framework.ScoreNormalizer {\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// NewBalancedAllocation initializes a new plugin and returns it.\nfunc NewBalancedAllocation(_ context.Context, baArgs runtime.Object, h framework.Handle, fts feature.Features) (framework.Plugin, error) {\n\targs, ok := baArgs.(*config.NodeResourcesBalancedAllocationArgs)\n\tif !ok {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"want args to be of type NodeResourcesBalancedAllocationArgs, got %T\", baArgs)\n\t}\n\n\tif err := validation.ValidateNodeResourcesBalancedAllocationArgs(nil, args); err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, err\n\t}\n\n\treturn &BalancedAllocation{\n\t\thandle: h,\n\t\tresourceAllocationScorer: resourceAllocationScorer{\n\t\t\tName:         BalancedAllocationName,\n\t\t\tscorer:       balancedResourceScorer,\n\t\t\tuseRequested: true,\n\t\t\tresources:    args.Resources,\n\t\t},\n\t}, nil\n}\n\nfunc balancedResourceScorer(requested, allocable []int64) int64 {\n\tvar resourceToFractions []float64\n\tvar totalFraction float64\n\tfor i := range requested {\n\t\tif allocable[i] == 0 {\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\t\tfraction := float64(requested[i]) / float64(allocable[i])\n\t\tif fraction > 1 {\n\t\t\tfraction = 1\n\t\t}\n\t\ttotalFraction += fraction\n\t\tresourceToFractions = append(resourceToFractions, fraction)\n\t}\n\n\tstd := 0.0\n\n\t// For most cases, resources are limited to cpu and memory, the std could be simplified to std := (fraction1-fraction2)/2\n\t// len(fractions) > 2: calculate std based on the well-known formula - root square of Σ((fraction(i)-mean)^2)/len(fractions)\n\t// Otherwise, set the std to zero is enough.\n\tif len(resourceToFractions) == 2 {\n\t\tstd = math.Abs((resourceToFractions[0] - resourceToFractions[1]) / 2)\n\n\t} else if len(resourceToFractions) > 2 {\n\t\tmean := totalFraction / float64(len(resourceToFractions))\n\t\tvar sum float64\n\t\tfor _, fraction := range resourceToFractions {\n\t\t\tsum = sum + (fraction-mean)*(fraction-mean)\n\t\t}\n\t\tstd = math.Sqrt(sum / float64(len(resourceToFractions)))\n\t}\n\n\t// STD (standard deviation) is always a positive value. 1-deviation lets the score to be higher for node which has least deviation and\n\t// multiplying it with `MaxNodeScore` provides the scaling factor needed.\n\treturn int64((1 - std) * float64(framework.MaxNodeScore))\n}\n"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":2,"cache_creation_input_tokens":3863,"cache_read_input_tokens":72510,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":3863},"output_tokens":1,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01USLfwmDaDcyrqUWgHcjXZn","session_id":"4e692961-63fc-432c-82cc-07b297ef7c6b","uuid":"268f180a-98ba-43f9-a7a1-c113a842e38c"}

Because init-containers run sequentially, we collect\n// the max in each dimension iteratively. In contrast, we sum the resource vectors for\n// regular containers since they run simultaneously.\n//\n// # The resources defined for Overhead should be added to the calculated Resource request sum\n//\n// Example:\n//\n// Pod:\n//\n//\tInitContainers\n//\t  IC1:\n//\t    CPU: 2\n//\t    Memory: 1G\n//\t  IC2:\n//\t    CPU: 2\n//\t    Memory: 3G\n//\tContainers\n//\t  C1:\n//\t    CPU: 2\n//\t    Memory: 1G\n//\t  C2:\n//\t    CPU: 1\n//\t    Memory: 1G\n//\n// Result: CPU: 3, Memory: 3G\nfunc computePodResourceRequest(pod *v1.Pod) *preFilterState {\n\t// pod hasn't scheduled yet so we don't need to worry about InPlacePodVerticalScalingEnabled\n\treqs := resource.PodRequests(pod, resource.PodResourcesOptions{})\n\tresult := &preFilterState{}\n\tresult.SetMaxResource(reqs)\n\treturn result\n}\n\n// PreFilter invoked at the prefilter extension point.\nfunc (f *Fit) PreFilter(ctx context.Context, cycleState *framework.CycleState, pod *v1.Pod) (*framework.PreFilterResult, *framework.Status) {\n\tif !f.enableSidecarContainers && hasRestartableInitContainer(pod) {\n\t\t// Scheduler will calculate resources usage for a Pod containing\n\t\t// restartable init containers that will be equal or more than kubelet will\n\t\t// require to run the Pod. So there will be no overbooking. However, to\n\t\t// avoid the inconsistency in resource calculation between the scheduler\n\t\t// and the older (before v1.28) kubelet, make the Pod unschedulable.\n\t\treturn nil, framework.NewStatus(framework.UnschedulableAndUnresolvable, \"Pod has a restartable init container and the SidecarContainers feature is disabled\")\n\t}\n\tcycleState.Write(preFilterStateKey, computePodResourceRequest(pod))\n\treturn nil, nil\n}\n\n// PreFilterExtensions returns prefilter extensions, pod add and remove.\nfunc (f *Fit) PreFilterExtensions() framework.PreFilterExtensions {\n\treturn nil\n}\n\nfunc getPreFilterState(cycleState *framework.CycleState) (*preFilterState, error) {\n\tc, err := cycleState.Read(preFilterStateKey)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\t// preFilterState doesn't exist, likely PreFilter wasn't invoked.\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"error reading %q from cycleState: %w\", preFilterStateKey, err)\n\t}\n\n\ts, ok := c.(*preFilterState)\n\tif !ok {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"%+v  convert to NodeResourcesFit.preFilterState error\", c)\n\t}\n\treturn s, nil\n}\n\n// EventsToRegister returns the possible events that may make a Pod\n// failed by this plugin schedulable.\nfunc (f *Fit) EventsToRegister() []framework.ClusterEventWithHint {\n\tpodActionType := framework.Delete\n\tif f.enableInPlacePodVerticalScaling {\n\t\t// If InPlacePodVerticalScaling (KEP 1287) is enabled, then PodUpdate event should be registered\n\t\t// for this plugin since a Pod update may free up resources that make other Pods schedulable.\n\t\tpodActionType |= framework.Update\n\t}\n\treturn []framework.ClusterEventWithHint{\n\t\t{Event: framework.ClusterEvent{Resource: framework.Pod, ActionType: podActionType}, QueueingHintFn: f.isSchedulableAfterPodChange},\n\t\t{Event: framework.ClusterEvent{Resource: framework.Node, ActionType: framework.Add | framework.Update}, QueueingHintFn: f.isSchedulableAfterNodeChange},\n\t}\n}\n\n// isSchedulableAfterPodChange is invoked whenever a pod deleted or updated. It checks whether\n// that change made a previously unschedulable pod schedulable.\nfunc (f *Fit) isSchedulableAfterPodChange(logger klog.Logger, pod *v1.Pod, oldObj, newObj interface{}) (framework.QueueingHint, error) {\n\toriginalPod, modifiedPod, err := schedutil.As[*v1.Pod](oldObj, newObj)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn framework.Queue, err\n\t}\n\n\tif modifiedPod == nil {\n\t\tif originalPod.Spec.NodeName == \"\" {\n\t\t\tlogger.V(5).Info(\"the deleted pod was unscheduled and it wouldn't make the unscheduled pod schedulable\", \"pod\", klog.KObj(pod), \"deletedPod\", klog.KObj(originalPod))\n\t\t\treturn framework.QueueSkip, nil\n\t\t}\n\t\tlogger.V(5).Info(\"another scheduled pod was deleted, and it may make the unscheduled pod schedulable\", \"pod\", klog.KObj(pod), \"deletedPod\", klog.KObj(originalPod))\n\t\treturn framework.Queue, nil\n\t}\n\n\tif !f.enableInPlacePodVerticalScaling {\n\t\t// If InPlacePodVerticalScaling (KEP 1287) is disabled, it cannot free up resources.\n\t\tlogger.V(5).Info(\"another pod was modified, but InPlacePodVerticalScaling is disabled, so it doesn't make the unscheduled pod schedulable\", \"pod\", klog.KObj(pod), \"modifiedPod\", klog.KObj(modifiedPod))\n\t\treturn framework.QueueSkip, nil\n\t}\n\n\t// Modifications may or may not be relevant. We only care about modifications that\n\t// change the other pod's resource request and the resource is also requested by the\n\t// pod we are trying to schedule.\n\tif !f.isResourceScaleDown(pod, originalPod, modifiedPod) {\n\t\tif loggerV := logger.V(10); loggerV.Enabled() {\n\t\t\t// Log more information.\n\t\t\tloggerV.Info(\"another Pod got modified, but the modification isn't related to the resource request\", \"pod\", klog.KObj(pod), \"modifiedPod\", klog.KObj(modifiedPod), \"diff\", cmp.Diff(originalPod, modifiedPod))\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\tlogger.V(5).Info(\"another Pod got modified, but the modification isn't related to the resource request\", \"pod\", klog.KObj(pod), \"modifiedPod\", klog.KObj(modifiedPod))\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn framework.QueueSkip, nil\n\t}\n\n\tlogger.V(5).Info(\"the max request resources of another scheduled pod got reduced and it may make the unscheduled pod schedulable\", \"pod\", klog.KObj(pod), \"modifiedPod\", klog.KObj(modifiedPod))\n\treturn framework.Queue, nil\n}\n\n// isResourceScaleDown checks whether the resource request of the modified pod is less than the original pod\n// for the resources requested by the pod we are trying to schedule.\nfunc (f *Fit) isResourceScaleDown(targetPod, originalOtherPod, modifiedOtherPod *v1.Pod) bool {\n\tif modifiedOtherPod.Spec.NodeName == \"\" {\n\t\t// no resource is freed up whatever the pod is modified.\n\t\treturn false\n\t}\n\n\t// the other pod was scheduled, so modification or deletion may free up some resources.\n\toriginalMaxResourceReq, modifiedMaxResourceReq := &framework.Resource{}, &framework.Resource{}\n\toriginalMaxResourceReq.SetMaxResource(resource.PodRequests(originalOtherPod, resource.PodResourcesOptions{InPlacePodVerticalScalingEnabled: f.enableInPlacePodVerticalScaling}))\n\tmodifiedMaxResourceReq.SetMaxResource(resource.PodRequests(modifiedOtherPod, resource.PodResourcesOptions{InPlacePodVerticalScalingEnabled: f.enableInPlacePodVerticalScaling}))\n\n\t// check whether the resource request of the modified pod is less than the original pod.\n\tpodRequests := resource.PodRequests(targetPod, resource.PodResourcesOptions{InPlacePodVerticalScalingEnabled: f.enableInPlacePodVerticalScaling})\n\tfor rName, rValue := range podRequests {\n\t\tif rValue.IsZero() {\n\t\t\t// We only care about the resources requested by the pod we are trying to schedule.\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\t\tswitch rName {\n\t\tcase v1.ResourceCPU:\n\t\t\tif originalMaxResourceReq.MilliCPU > modifiedMaxResourceReq.MilliCPU {\n\t\t\t\treturn true\n\t\t\t}\n\t\tcase v1.ResourceMemory:\n\t\t\tif originalMaxResourceReq.Memory > modifiedMaxResourceReq.Memory {\n\t\t\t\treturn true\n\t\t\t}\n\t\tcase v1.ResourceEphemeralStorage:\n\t\t\tif originalMaxResourceReq.EphemeralStorage > modifiedMaxResourceReq.EphemeralStorage {\n\t\t\t\treturn true\n\t\t\t}\n\t\tdefault:\n\t\t\tif schedutil.IsScalarResourceName(rName) && originalMaxResourceReq.ScalarResources[rName] > modifiedMaxResourceReq.ScalarResources[rName] {\n\t\t\t\treturn true\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn false\n}\n\n// isSchedulableAfterNodeChange is invoked whenever a node added or changed. It checks whether\n// that change made a previously unschedulable pod schedulable.\nfunc (f *Fit) isSchedulableAfterNodeChange(logger klog.Logger, pod *v1.Pod, oldObj, newObj interface{}) (framework.QueueingHint, error) {\n\t_, modifiedNode, err := schedutil.As[*v1.Node](oldObj, newObj)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn framework.Queue, err\n\t}\n\t// TODO: also check if the original node meets the pod's resource requestments once preCheck is completely removed.\n\t// See: https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/issues/110175\n\tif isFit(pod, modifiedNode) {\n\t\tlogger.V(5).Info(\"node was updated, and may fit with the pod's resource requestments\", \"pod\", klog.KObj(pod), \"node\", klog.KObj(modifiedNode))\n\t\treturn framework.Queue, nil\n\t}\n\n\tlogger.V(5).Info(\"node was created or updated, but it doesn't have enough resource(s) to accommodate this pod\", \"pod\", klog.KObj(pod), \"node\", klog.KObj(modifiedNode))\n\treturn framework.QueueSkip, nil\n}\n\n// isFit checks if the pod fits the node. If the node is nil, it returns false.\n// It constructs a fake NodeInfo object for the node and checks if the pod fits the node.\nfunc isFit(pod *v1.Pod, node *v1.Node) bool {\n\tif node == nil {\n\t\treturn false\n\t}\n\tnodeInfo := framework.NewNodeInfo()\n\tnodeInfo.SetNode(node)\n\treturn len(Fits(pod, nodeInfo)) == 0\n}\n\n// Filter invoked at the filter extension point.\n// Checks if a node has sufficient resources, such as cpu, memory, gpu, opaque int resources etc to run a pod.\n// It returns a list of insufficient resources, if empty, then the node has all the resources requested by the pod.\nfunc (f *Fit) Filter(ctx context.Context, cycleState *framework.CycleState, pod *v1.Pod, nodeInfo *framework.NodeInfo) *framework.Status {\n\ts, err := getPreFilterState(cycleState)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn framework.AsStatus(err)\n\t}\n\n\tinsufficientResources := fitsRequest(s, nodeInfo, f.ignoredResources, f.ignoredResourceGroups)\n\n\tif len(insufficientResources) != 0 {\n\t\t// We will keep all failure reasons.\n\t\tfailureReasons := make([]string, 0, len(insufficientResources))\n\t\tfor i := range insufficientResources {\n\t\t\tfailureReasons = append(failureReasons, insufficientResources[i].Reason)\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn framework.NewStatus(framework.Unschedulable, failureReasons...)\n\t}\n\treturn nil\n}\n\nfunc hasRestartableInitContainer(pod *v1.Pod) bool {\n\tfor _, c := range pod.Spec.InitContainers {\n\t\tif c.RestartPolicy != nil && *c.RestartPolicy == v1.ContainerRestartPolicyAlways {\n\t\t\treturn true\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn false\n}\n\n// InsufficientResource describes what kind of resource limit is hit and caused the pod to not fit the node.\ntype InsufficientResource struct {\n\tResourceName v1.ResourceName\n\t// We explicitly have a parameter for reason to avoid formatting a message on the fly\n\t// for common resources, which is expensive for cluster autoscaler simulations.\n\tReason    string\n\tRequested int64\n\tUsed      int64\n\tCapacity  int64\n}\n\n// Fits checks if node have enough resources to host the pod.\nfunc Fits(pod *v1.Pod, nodeInfo *framework.NodeInfo) []InsufficientResource {\n\treturn fitsRequest(computePodResourceRequest(pod), nodeInfo, nil, nil)\n}\n\nfunc fitsRequest(podRequest *preFilterState, nodeInfo *framework.NodeInfo, ignoredExtendedResources, ignoredResourceGroups sets.Set[string]) []InsufficientResource {\n\tinsufficientResources := make([]InsufficientResource, 0, 4)\n\n\tallowedPodNumber := nodeInfo.Allocatable.AllowedPodNumber\n\tif len(nodeInfo.Pods)+1 > allowedPodNumber {\n\t\tinsufficientResources = append(insufficientResources, InsufficientResource{\n\t\t\tResourceName: v1.ResourcePods,\n\t\t\tReason:       \"Too many pods\",\n\t\t\tRequested:    1,\n\t\t\tUsed:         int64(len(nodeInfo.Pods)),\n\t\t\tCapacity:     int64(allowedPodNumber),\n\t\t})\n\t}\n\n\tif podRequest.MilliCPU == 0 &&\n\t\tpodRequest.Memory == 0 &&\n\t\tpodRequest.EphemeralStorage == 0 &&\n\t\tlen(podRequest.ScalarResources) == 0 {\n\t\treturn insufficientResources\n\t}\n\n\tif podRequest.MilliCPU > 0 && podRequest.MilliCPU > (nodeInfo.Allocatable.MilliCPU-nodeInfo.Requested.MilliCPU) {\n\t\tinsufficientResources = append(insufficientResources, InsufficientResource{\n\t\t\tResourceName: v1.ResourceCPU,\n\t\t\tReason:       \"Insufficient cpu\",\n\t\t\tRequested:    podRequest.MilliCPU,\n\t\t\tUsed:         nodeInfo.Requested.MilliCPU,\n\t\t\tCapacity:     nodeInfo.Allocatable.MilliCPU,\n\t\t})\n\t}\n\tif podRequest.Memory > 0 && podRequest.Memory > (nodeInfo.Allocatable.Memory-nodeInfo.Requested.Memory) {\n\t\tinsufficientResources = append(insufficientResources, InsufficientResource{\n\t\t\tResourceName: v1.ResourceMemory,\n\t\t\tReason:       \"Insufficient memory\",\n\t\t\tRequested:    podRequest.Memory,\n\t\t\tUsed:         nodeInfo.Requested.Memory,\n\t\t\tCapacity:     nodeInfo.Allocatable.Memory,\n\t\t})\n\t}\n\tif podRequest.EphemeralStorage > 0 &&\n\t\tpodRequest.EphemeralStorage > (nodeInfo.Allocatable.EphemeralStorage-nodeInfo.Requested.EphemeralStorage) {\n\t\tinsufficientResources = append(insufficientResources, InsufficientResource{\n\t\t\tResourceName: v1.ResourceEphemeralStorage,\n\t\t\tReason:       \"Insufficient ephemeral-storage\",\n\t\t\tRequested:    podRequest.EphemeralStorage,\n\t\t\tUsed:         nodeInfo.Requested.EphemeralStorage,\n\t\t\tCapacity:     nodeInfo.Allocatable.EphemeralStorage,\n\t\t})\n\t}\n\n\tfor rName, rQuant := range podRequest.ScalarResources {\n\t\t// Skip in case request quantity is zero\n\t\tif rQuant == 0 {\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif v1helper.IsExtendedResourceName(rName) {\n\t\t\t// If this resource is one of the extended resources that should be ignored, we will skip checking it.\n\t\t\t// rName is guaranteed to have a slash due to API validation.\n\t\t\tvar rNamePrefix string\n\t\t\tif ignoredResourceGroups.Len() > 0 {\n\t\t\t\trNamePrefix = strings.Split(string(rName), \"/\")[0]\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tif ignoredExtendedResources.Has(string(rName)) || ignoredResourceGroups.Has(rNamePrefix) {\n\t\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif rQuant > (nodeInfo.Allocatable.ScalarResources[rName] - nodeInfo.Requested.ScalarResources[rName]) {\n\t\t\tinsufficientResources = append(insufficientResources, InsufficientResource{\n\t\t\t\tResourceName: rName,\n\t\t\t\tReason:       fmt.Sprintf(\"Insufficient %v\", rName),\n\t\t\t\tRequested:    podRequest.ScalarResources[rName],\n\t\t\t\tUsed:         nodeInfo.Requested.ScalarResources[rName],\n\t\t\t\tCapacity:     nodeInfo.Allocatable.ScalarResources[rName],\n\t\t\t})\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn insufficientResources\n}\n\n// Score invoked at the Score extension point.\nfunc (f *Fit) Score(ctx context.Context, state *framework.CycleState, pod *v1.Pod, nodeName string) (int64, *framework.Status) {\n\tnodeInfo, err := f.handle.SnapshotSharedLister().NodeInfos().Get(nodeName)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn 0, framework.AsStatus(fmt.Errorf(\"getting node %q from Snapshot: %w\", nodeName, err))\n\t}\n\n\ts, err := getPreScoreState(state)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\ts = &preScoreState{\n\t\t\tpodRequests: f.calculatePodResourceRequestList(pod, f.resources),\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn f.score(ctx, pod, nodeInfo, Core Interface Definition (interface.go)\n\n### Before:\n```go\n// ScoreExtensions is an interface for Score extended functionality.\ntype ScoreExtensions interface {\n\t// NormalizeScore is called for all node scores produced by the same plugin's \"Score\"\n\t// method. A successful run of NormalizeScore will update the scores list and return\n\t// a success status.\n\tNormalizeScore(ctx context.Context, state *CycleState, p *v1.Pod, scores NodeScoreList) *Status\n}\n\n// ScorePlugin is an interface that must be implemented by \"Score\" plugins to rank\n// nodes that passed the filtering phase.\ntype ScorePlugin interface {\n\tPlugin\n\t// Score is called on each filtered node. It must return success and an integer\n\t// indicating the rank of the node. All scoring plugins must return success or\n\t// the pod will be rejected.\n\tScore(ctx context.Context, state *CycleState, p *v1.Pod, nodeName string) (int64, *Status)\n\n\t// ScoreExtensions returns a ScoreExtensions interface if it implements one, or nil if does not.\n\tScoreExtensions() ScoreExtensions\n}\n```\n\n### After:\n```go\n// ScoreNormalizer is an interface for Score extended functionality.\ntype ScoreNormalizer interface {\n\t// NormalizeScore is called for all node scores produced by the same plugin's \"Score\"\n\t// method. A successful run of NormalizeScore will update the scores list and return\n\t// a success status.\n\tNormalizeScore(ctx context.Context, state *CycleState, p *v1.Pod, scores NodeScoreList) *Status\n}\n\n// ScorePlugin is an interface that must be implemented by \"Score\" plugins to rank\n// nodes that passed the filtering phase.\ntype ScorePlugin interface {\n\tPlugin\n\t// Score is called on each filtered node. It must return success and an integer\n\t// indicating the rank of the node. All scoring plugins must return success or\n\t// the pod will be rejected.\n\tScore(ctx context.Context, state *CycleState, p *v1.Pod, nodeName string) (int64, *Status)\n\n\t// ScoreNormalizer returns a ScoreNormalizer interface if it implements one, or nil if does not.\n\tScoreNormalizer() ScoreNormalizer\n}\n```\n\n**Key Changes:**\n- Interface name: `ScoreExtensions` → `ScoreNormalizer`\n- Method name: `ScoreExtensions()` → `ScoreNormalizer()`\n- Return type: `ScoreExtensions` → `ScoreNormalizer`\n- Updated comments to reflect new naming\n\n---\n\n## 2. Metrics Constants (metrics.go)\n\n### Before:\n```go\nconst (\n\tPreFilter                   = \"PreFilter\"\n\tFilter                      = \"Filter\"\n\tPreFilterExtensionAddPod    = \"PreFilterExtensionAddPod\"\n\tPreFilterExtensionRemovePod = \"PreFilterExtensionRemovePod\"\n\tPostFilter                  = \"PostFilter\"\n\tPreScore                    = \"PreScore\"\n\tScore                       = \"Score\"\n\tScoreExtensionNormalize     = \"ScoreExtensionNormalize\"\n\tPreBind                     = \"PreBind\"\n\tBind                        = \"Bind\"\n\tPostBind                    = \"PostBind\"\n)\n```\n\n### After:\n```go\nconst (\n\tPreFilter                   = \"PreFilter\"\n\tFilter                      = \"Filter\"\n\tPreFilterExtensionAddPod    = \"PreFilterExtensionAddPod\"\n\tPreFilterExtensionRemovePod = \"PreFilterExtensionRemovePod\"\n\tPostFilter                  = \"PostFilter\"\n\tPreScore                    = \"PreScore\"\n\tScore                       = \"Score\"\n\tScoreNormalize              = \"ScoreNormalize\"\n\tPreBind                     = \"PreBind\"\n\tBind                        = \"Bind\"\n\tPostBind                    = \"PostBind\"\n)\n```\n\n**Key Changes:**\n- Constant name: `ScoreExtensionNormalize` → `ScoreNormalize`\n- Constant value: `\"ScoreExtensionNormalize\"` → `\"ScoreNormalize\"`\n\n---\n\n## 3. metrics.SinceInSeconds(startTime))\n\treturn status\n}\n```\n\n### After:\n```go\n// Run NormalizeScore method for each ScorePlugin in parallel.\nf.Parallelizer().Until(ctx, len(plugins), func(index framework.NodeScoreList) *framework.Status {\n\tif !state.ShouldRecordPluginMetrics() {\n\t\treturn pl.ScoreNormalizer().NormalizeScore(ctx, state, pod, nodeScoreList)\n\t}\n\tstartTime := time.Now()\n\tstatus := pl.ScoreNormalizer().NormalizeScore(ctx, state, pod, nodeScoreList)\n\tf.metricsRecorder.ObservePluginDurationAsync(metrics.ScoreNormalize, pl.Name(), status.Code().String(), metrics.SinceInSeconds(startTime))\n\treturn status\n}\n```\n\n**Key Changes:**\n- Method calls: `pl.ScoreExtensions()` → `pl.ScoreNormalizer()`\n- Function name: `runScoreExtension()` → Plugin Implementation (example: taint_toleration.go)\n\n### Before:\n```go\n// NormalizeScore invoked after scoring all nodes.\nfunc (pl *TaintToleration) NormalizeScore(ctx context.Context, _ *framework.CycleState, pod *v1.Pod, scores framework.NodeScoreList) *framework.Status {\n\treturn helper.DefaultNormalizeScore(framework.MaxNodeScore, true, scores)\n}\n\n// ScoreExtensions of the Score plugin.\nfunc (pl *TaintToleration) ScoreExtensions() framework.ScoreExtensions {\n\treturn pl\n}\n```\n\n### After:\n```go\n// NormalizeScore invoked after scoring all nodes.\nfunc (pl *TaintToleration) NormalizeScore(ctx context.Context, _ *framework.CycleState, pod *v1.Pod, scores framework.NodeScoreList) *framework.Status {\n\treturn helper.DefaultNormalizeScore(framework.MaxNodeScore, true, scores)\n}\n\n// ScoreNormalizer of the Score plugin.\nfunc (pl *TaintToleration) ScoreNormalizer() framework.ScoreNormalizer {\n\treturn pl\n}\n```\n\n**Key Changes:**\n- Method name: `ScoreExtensions()` → `ScoreNormalizer()`\n- Return type: `framework.ScoreExtensions` → `framework.ScoreNormalizer`\n- Updated comment\n\n---\n\n## 5. scheduler framework that renames the `ScoreExtensions` interface to `ScoreNormalizer` to better reflect its single purpose: normalizing scores produced by score plugins.\n\n**Why?** The `ScoreExtensions` interface contains only one method (`NormalizeScore`), making the name misleadingly generic. `ScoreNormalizer` accurately describes what the interface does.\n\n---\n\n## ✅ Project
